scenario_name: Voice in-vehicle infotainment assistant with cloud + on-device hybrid
description: An OEM deploys a voice assistant in vehicle infotainment systems. Light queries (climate, music, navigation) run on-device; complex queries (point-of-interest lookup, calendar) round-trip to cloud. The assistant accesses the driver's calendar, contacts, and message read-aloud through a phone-integration tier. Voice biometrics are captured for personalization; cabin audio (potentially picking up passenger conversation) is in scope.
assumptions: Driver consent captured at pairing; passenger consent not separately captured.
On-device model handles the bulk of queries; cloud round-trip required for ~20% of requests.
Geographic deployment includes EU and California jurisdictions.

components.description: On-device small LLM + cloud LLM hybrid.
Phone integration: contacts, calendar, messaging read-aloud.
Voice biometrics for driver personalization; cabin audio buffer.
Wake-word verification, content-classification on cabin audio.
Aggregated telemetry (no raw audio retention) for product analytics.

risks_identified.rationale: Calendar, contacts, and messages read-aloud expose PII; cabin audio may inadvertently capture passenger conversation.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Driver consent captured; passenger consent gap remains; on-device retention only for raw audio.

risks_identified.rationale: GDPR (consent specificity), CCPA (driver biometrics), EU AI Act biometrics provisions, state biometric-information laws (BIPA-class).
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Privacy notices in place; passenger-consent gap is a known compliance risk.

risks_identified.rationale: Multimodal capture: audio plus on-screen content; risk of adversarial voice commands designed to spoof identification.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Wake-word verification; voiceprint check for sensitive actions; no protection from replay attacks.

risks_identified.rationale: Shared vehicle (rental, car-share) cross-user context leakage if personalization not properly reset.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Reset on key-fob pairing change; rental-mode option exists but is operator-opt-in.

risks_identified.rationale: Telemetry pipeline aggregates voice-feature data into cloud analytics.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: No raw audio retention; aggregation only; opt-out path available.

risks_identified.rationale: In-vehicle assistant has broad reach into paired phone data while driver is unable to monitor in real time.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Driver-only configuration UI; phone-integration scope user-configurable.

existing_controls.description: On-device-only retention of raw audio; no raw audio leaves the vehicle.
Wake-word verification + voiceprint check for sensitive actions.
Personalization reset on key-fob pairing change.
Driver-only configuration UI; phone-integration scope user-configurable.

control_gaps.description: Passenger consent for cabin audio not explicitly captured.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add a one-time cabin-audio-aware notice and a session-mute option discoverable to passengers.

control_gaps.description: No protection against replay attacks on voiceprint authentication.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add liveness checks or challenge-response for sensitive voiceprint-authenticated actions.

control_gaps.description: Rental-mode is operator-opt-in; many shared vehicles do not enable it.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Detect rental fleet via VIN-list; enable rental-mode by default; require operator opt-out.
